name: NBC (NY) Employees Federal Credit Union
description_short: NBC (NY) Employees FCU now operates as XCEL Federal Credit Union, serving members in New York, New Jersey, and nationwide with banking and loan products.
description_long:
NBC (NY) Employees Federal Credit Union has rebranded and now operates under the name XCEL Federal Credit Union. The organization has been in business for approximately 80 years, indicating a long institutional history serving working Americans originally tied to NBC's New York employee base. The credit union's website (nbcnyefcu.org) now redirects fully to XCELfcu.org, suggesting a complete merger or rebrand rather than a simple name change. The NJ-based phone number (973-275-9235) indicates primary operations are headquartered in New Jersey while serving members across New York, New Jersey, and the broader country.

XCEL Federal Credit Union offers a range of consumer financial products. Core offerings include various loan types accessible through a centralized loan application portal, debt consolidation products marketed as a way to break from debt, and the Family Security Plan, which bundles premium insurance benefits for members. Members can access accounts through online banking and schedule in-person appointments. Live chat support is available Monday through Friday, 9am to 5pm ET.

What distinguishes this institution is its longevity — roughly 80 years in operation — and its dual-state footprint serving both New York and New Jersey members. The credit union emphasizes affordability, describing its mission as providing 'high-quality, affordable products and services.' The Family Security Plan offering is a notable differentiator, bundling insurance benefits in a way that typical banks do not. The fraud alert prominently displayed on the homepage reflects active member communication around security.

Honestly, the public-facing website provides limited detail on rates, fees, membership eligibility requirements, or specific loan terms. Prospective members who are not current or former NBC employees or affiliates may find membership eligibility unclear. The asset size counter on the homepage was animated and unresolved in the scraped content, so exact financials cannot be confirmed. Members seeking full transparency on APRs or product terms will need to contact the credit union directly or log into the member portal.
address: 1221 6th Ave level c2, New York, NY 10020

company_info.headquarters: 1221 6th Ave level c2, New York, NY 10020

pros: Approximately 80 years in operation — established institutional history
Serves members across New York, New Jersey, and nationwide
Centralized loan application portal to apply and check status in one place
Family Security Plan offers bundled insurance benefits — uncommon at most banks
Debt consolidation product specifically marketed with clear member benefit framing
Live chat support available Mon–Fri 9am–5pm ET
Proactive fraud alert communication on homepage — warns members about impersonation scams
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly stated on the public website
No rates, APRs, or fee schedules visible without logging in
Website redirects from nbcnyefcu.org to XCELfcu.org — the rebrand may cause confusion for longtime members
Limited online self-service information — appointment or phone contact appears required for most inquiries
Asset size and member count not clearly disclosed on the public site
best_for: Current or former NBC (NY) employees or affiliates eligible for XCEL FCU membership
NY/NJ residents seeking a credit union alternative to traditional banks with a long track record
Consumers looking to consolidate debt through a member-owned institution
Members interested in bundled insurance benefits alongside banking products
diagnosis: XCEL Federal Credit Union (formerly NBC NY Employees FCU) is best suited for current or former NBC employees and their families who qualify for membership and want a long-established credit union with competitive loan products and bundled insurance. The main caveat is that membership eligibility, rates, and product terms are not publicly disclosed — prospective members must contact the institution directly to determine if they qualify and whether the products are competitive with alternatives.
services: Consumer loans (multiple types via unified application)
Fraud monitoring and member security alerts
